note: Shared server+UI fixture for the title-prefix -> `kind` column migration (decision e852fb0c, stage B). Predicates read `kind`; `title` is retained ONLY to prove stage-A backfill parity via kindFromTitle(title) === kind. Do not add cases whose title and kind disagree - no such row can exist in the DB.

cases.name: the Inbox epic is an epic by kind, not by the word inbox
cases.title: [EPIC] Inbox
cases.label: [EPIC]

cases.name: leaf whose title merely CONTAINS the word inbox (bucketEpic topic-vs-role trap)
cases.title: Fix inbox planning-only guard

cases.name: leaf whose title MENTIONS a role prefix mid-string - never a role
cases.title: Stop reading [EPIC] out of titles

cases.name: leading whitespace is tolerated (regex ^\s*, SQL TRIM)
cases.title: [EPIC] leading spaces
cases.label: [EPIC]

cases.name: prefix match is case-insensitive (regex /i, SQLite LIKE)
cases.title: [epic] lowercase prefix
cases.label: [EPIC]

cases.name: mission wins over epic when checked in order (kindFromTitle checks mission first)
cases.title: [MISSION] [EPIC] ambiguous double prefix
cases.label: [MISSION]

cases.name: empty title is a leaf (kindFromTitle is TOTAL - never null)
null title is a leaf (predicates accept string | null | undefined)
stage C preview: a mission with its prefix already stripped is STILL a mission by kind
cases.title: Converge the build loop
cases.label: [MISSION]

cases.name: stage C preview: a stripped epic is STILL an epic by kind
cases.title: Escalation briefing
cases.label: [EPIC]
